companies.name: NVIDIA
companies.summary: NVDA is in summer-swoon territory after pulling back from its May 18 all-time high, but analysts cite five catalysts for a return to $300 by year-end: Vera Rubin volume ramp, Jalapeño/Stargate demand, a new revenue-sharing AI factory model, global data center buildout, and government AI adoption. Sharon AI is the first live example of NVIDIA's new revenue-sharing infrastructure model, deploying up to 40,000 GB300 GPUs. Tesla's Q2 delivery beat and Fable 5 restoration accelerate Vera Rubin NVL72 utilization across CoreWeave and SpaceX Colossus.

items.headline: NVIDIA unveils revenue-sharing AI factory model — Sharon AI first deployer with 40,000 GB300 GPUs
items.detail: NVIDIA is partnering with AI clouds under a new revenue-sharing and credit-support business model that aligns economics between NVIDIA and cloud providers. Sharon AI and Firmus are the first companies operating under this model, with Sharon AI deploying up to 40,000 NVIDIA Grace Blackwell GB300 GPUs. The model shifts NVIDIA from a hardware vendor to an AI factory infrastructure co-investor, deepening long-term deployment relationships beyond one-time chip sales.

items.headline: NVDA summer swoon — five 2026 catalysts cited for potential $300 target; $485B U.S. GDP impact
items.detail: 247 Wall Street outlined five catalysts for NVDA to recover and reach $300 by end of 2026: Vera Rubin volume ramp in H2, Jalapeño/Stargate demand from the OpenAI-Broadcom custom silicon program, the new revenue-sharing AI factory model, global data center buildout, and government AI adoption via the Palantir Sovereign AI OS. Public First estimates NVIDIA-driven AI demand will contribute $485 billion to U.S. GDP in 2026 and support more than 100,000 jobs. NVIDIA runs 81% of TOP500 supercomputers with 90% of new entries built on NVIDIA silicon.

items.headline: CoreWeave Vera Rubin NVL72 at full utilization — Fable 5 restoration clears Claude workload backlog
items.detail: CoreWeave completed the first Vera Rubin NVL72 production validation on June 1 (4x training and 10x inference-per-watt improvements) and Fable 5's global restoration on July 1 cleared the 19-day Claude workload backlog on those clusters. A record 35 European Vera Rubin HPC supercomputers are in development across European research institutions as confirmed at ISC High Performance 2026 in Hamburg. NVIDIA BioNeMo Agent Toolkit launched at ISC to enable AI agents to accelerate scientific discovery.

companies.name: Alphabet / Google DeepMind
companies.category: Foundation models, search, cloud, and AI research
companies.summary: Gemini 3.5 Pro remains unreleased as of July 3 with no specific date announced within July. Prediction markets are now actively tracking the probability of a July 31 deadline. Gemini 3.5 Flash is expected as the free/low-cost tier before Pro becomes generally available. GOOGL is trading around $357, down approximately 12% from its May 18 all-time high of $408.61. Alphabet entered the Dow Jones Industrial Average on June 29. DeepMind's talent exodus continues to weigh on sentiment and Apple's September Siri AI launch timeline.

items.headline: Gemini 3.5 Pro still unreleased July 3 — prediction market now tracking July 31 deadline
items.detail: Gemini 3.5 Pro remains unreleased as of July 3, 2026. Google has not confirmed a specific date within July. Manifold prediction markets are now actively tracking the probability of a release before July 31. Gemini 3.5 Flash is expected to serve as the free/low-cost workhorse tier, with 3.5 Pro as a premium paid offering. The continued delay compounds Apple's Siri AI delivery risk heading toward the September iPhone launch and reinforces DeepMind talent exodus concerns.

items.headline: Alphabet joins Dow Jones Industrial Average — GOOGL at ~$357; Q1 Cloud revenue +63% YoY, $460B backlog
items.detail: Alphabet officially entered the DJIA on June 29, replacing Verizon, and rose approximately 4% on the inclusion. Q1 2026 Google Cloud revenue was up 63% year over year with a contracted backlog of $460 billion. GOOGL trades around $357 on July 3, down approximately 12% from its May 18 all-time high of $408.61. The Dow inclusion signals institutional commitment to Alphabet as a benchmark holding despite near-term model delivery concerns.

items.headline: DeepMind talent exodus: Shazeer to OpenAI, Jumper active at Anthropic — five senior researchers departed
items.detail: Noam Shazeer — Gemini co-lead and Transformer paper co-author — departed DeepMind for OpenAI in June 2026. John Jumper (Nobel laureate, AlphaFold2 co-creator) is now active at Anthropic, appearing at the Claude Science launch event on June 30. Three other senior researchers also departed (Jonas Adler, Alexander Pritzel, Arthur Conmy). The talent exodus cost Alphabet approximately $225 billion in market cap during a single-day 5% drop in June and remains the dominant narrative risk for Google's AI model execution.

companies.name: Microsoft
companies.category: Cloud AI, copilots, and enterprise software
companies.summary: Microsoft launched Microsoft Frontier Co. on July 2 — a $2.5 billion subsidiary with 6,000 forward-deployed engineers to accelerate enterprise AI adoption, following Amazon's $1 billion FDE initiative by two days. Copilot Cowork usage-based billing is in its third day. Microsoft 365 with Copilot pricing increases are in full effect. Claude models in Microsoft Foundry on NVIDIA GB300 Blackwell Ultra are generally available. Microsoft is reportedly evaluating DeepSeek V4 as a cost-efficient Copilot Cowork option.

items.headline: Microsoft Frontier Co. launched — $2.5B, 6,000 forward-deployed engineers for enterprise AI adoption
items.detail: Microsoft launched Microsoft Frontier Co. on July 2 — a new $2.5 billion subsidiary with approximately 6,000 employees embedded with enterprise customers for six-to-twelve-month engagements. Led by Rodrigo Kede Lima (former Microsoft Asia head), the division draws from existing FDEs, technical consultants, and industry specialists. Staff operate as virtual employees — attending standups, writing code, and training citizen developers. The launch follows Amazon's $1 billion FDE initiative from June 30 and similar programs from OpenAI and Anthropic launched in May.

items.headline: Copilot Cowork billing active day 3 — Microsoft 365 with Copilot pricing in full commercial effect
items.detail: Microsoft Copilot Cowork usage-based billing at $0.01 per Copilot Credit is active following the July 1 go-live, with the Frontier tier supporting Anthropic Opus 4.8, Sonnet 4.6, and OpenAI GPT-5.5. Microsoft 365 Business Standard with Copilot ($23.50/user/month) and Business Premium with Copilot ($32/user/month) are now permanent standard commercial SKUs. Microsoft is reportedly evaluating DeepSeek V4 as a cost-efficient model option within Copilot Cowork alongside OpenAI and Anthropic models.

items.headline: Claude models in Microsoft Foundry GA on GB300 Blackwell Ultra — Maia 200 negotiations ongoing
items.detail: Claude models in Microsoft Foundry — hosted on Azure and running on NVIDIA GB300 Blackwell Ultra GPUs — are generally available as of June 29. Microsoft plans a 40% GB300 capacity expansion by September 2026. Microsoft and Anthropic remain in active negotiations to deploy Claude on Maia 200 custom AI accelerators. Frontier Co.'s launch means enterprise customers can now get embedded AI adoption help using both Claude and GPT-5.5 through Microsoft's own professional services arm.

companies.name: OpenAI
companies.summary: GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna remain restricted to approximately 20 government-vetted organizations on July 3 — day 7 of the limited preview. Broader access is expected in the July 10–17 window per Sam Altman's stated intent. OpenAI confirmed GPT-5.6 Sol will deploy on Cerebras wafer-scale hardware this month at up to 750 tokens per second. The Apple breach-of-contract dispute remains unresolved with outside counsel retained. ChatGPT's global AI assistant market share is at 46.4% — the first time below 50%.

items.headline: GPT-5.6 Sol broader access window July 10–17 approaching — government restrictions continue day 7
items.detail: GPT-5.6 Sol ($5/$30 per million input/output tokens), Terra ($2.50/$15), and Luna ($1/$6) remain in government-staged limited preview as of July 3, restricted to approximately 20 organizations under a White House executive order requiring staged deployment benchmarking. Sam Altman told staff he hopes to release GPT-5.6 broadly around July 10–17. GPT-5.5 remains fully available. GPT-4.5 was retired June 26 and replaced by GPT-5.5 in existing conversations. ChatGPT global AI assistant market share: 46.4% (Gemini 27.7%, Claude 10.3%).

items.headline: GPT-5.6 Sol on Cerebras this month at 750 tok/sec — OpenAI-Cerebras $20B+ deal powering inference
items.detail: OpenAI confirmed plans to deploy GPT-5.6 Sol on Cerebras wafer-scale hardware this month, targeting up to 750 tokens per second — dramatically faster than GPU-based inference. The multi-year OpenAI-Cerebras compute deal covers 750 megawatts of inference capacity valued at more than $20 billion, with an option to scale to $30 billion. Codex-Spark on Cerebras already achieves more than 1,000 tokens per second for coding tasks. Cerebras Q1 2026 revenue was $193.4M (+92% YoY), massively beating the $56.65M estimate.

items.headline: Apple breach-of-contract dispute still pending — outside counsel retained; iOS 27 Extensions disabled pending EU DMA
items.detail: OpenAI retained outside counsel and continues to weigh a formal breach-of-contract notice against Apple over ChatGPT's perceived lack of prime placement in the rebuilt Siri AI. No lawsuit has been filed as of July 3. The dispute arose after Apple's June 8 WWDC announcement that Google Gemini powers the rebuilt Siri AI in iOS 27. The iOS 27 Extensions framework (user choice of ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini inside Siri) remains disabled pending EU DMA regulatory clarity. Both sides prefer an out-of-court resolution.

companies.name: Anthropic
companies.summary: Anthropic launched the Claude apps gateway on July 2 — a self-hosted control plane for Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock and Google Cloud with SSO, policy enforcement, per-user cost tracking, and multi-provider failover. Claude Enterprise also gained richer admin analytics, model-level entitlements, and spend alerts. Claude Sonnet 5 is in its third day as the default for Free and Pro plans. Claude Science grant applications remain open through July 15. October 2026 Nasdaq IPO target intact with $65B Series H at $965B post-money valuation and $47B ARR.

items.headline: Claude apps gateway launched — self-hosted Claude Code control plane for Bedrock and Google Cloud
items.detail: Anthropic launched the Claude apps gateway — a single stateless container organizations deploy on their own infrastructure (backed by PostgreSQL) to centrally manage Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock or Google Cloud. Key features: OIDC-compliant SSO (Google Workspace, Microsoft Entra ID, Okta), per-user and per-org spend caps, policy enforcement, and optional multi-provider failover between Bedrock and Google Cloud. Inference traffic and usage data stay on the organization's infrastructure and do not flow to Anthropic unless explicitly configured to use the Claude API.

items.headline: Claude Enterprise gains admin analytics, model entitlements, and spend alerts; 28 new security integrations
items.detail: Anthropic added richer admin analytics, model-level entitlements, and spend alerts to Claude Enterprise, giving IT administrators deeper visibility into usage, cost, and productivity trends. Admins can track and cap spending at the model level and receive alerts before budgets are exceeded. Anthropic also expanded Claude's enterprise security governance with 28 new integrations. The updates complement the Claude apps gateway to give enterprise customers a complete management layer for Claude Code and API usage across teams.

items.headline: Sonnet 5 as default day 3; Claude Science grants open through July 15; October Nasdaq IPO track intact
items.detail: July 3 is the third full day Claude Sonnet 5 serves as the default model for Anthropic's Free and Pro plans. Sonnet 5 offers performance approaching Opus 4.8 at roughly 60% lower cost, with introductory pricing of $2/$10 per million tokens through August 31. Claude Science — a research AI workbench in beta for Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise users — has grant applications open for up to $30,000 in Claude credits for 50 selected projects through July 15; awards announced July 31. Series H: $65B at $965B post-money; ARR: $47B; October 2026 Nasdaq IPO target with Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, and Morgan Stanley.

companies.name: Meta
companies.category: Open models, consumer AI, and infrastructure
companies.summary: Meta Compute cloud coverage continued through July 3 with Motley Fool analysis positioning Meta as a rival to Amazon and Microsoft. META stock is consolidating near $612 after the 9–10% surge on July 1. Meta's 2026 capex guidance stands at $125–145 billion (raised from $115–135B in April Q1 earnings). CoreWeave's $21B take-or-pay contract through 2032 is protected even as Meta builds competing cloud capacity. Digitimes noted server supply chain contacts are not seeing changes to Meta's 2026 shipment commitments.

items.headline: Meta Compute cloud analysis continues July 3 — Motley Fool positions Meta vs. Amazon/Microsoft
items.detail: Motley Fool's July 3 analysis describes Meta Compute as a two-pronged cloud business: selling hosted AI model API access (similar to AWS Bedrock) and selling raw GPU compute capacity (similar to CoreWeave). The division is led by Santosh Janardhan (infrastructure head), Daniel Gross (Meta Superintelligence Labs), and Dina Powell McCormick (Meta President). Meta raised its 2026 capex guidance to $125–145 billion in April — nearly double its 2025 spend of $72 billion — signing approximately $107 billion in new multi-year infrastructure commitments in a single quarter.

items.headline: Digitimes: Meta's 2026 supply chain demand unchanged despite cloud competition news
items.detail: Digitimes reported on July 3 that server supply chain contacts are not seeing changes to Meta's 2026 shipment and revenue commitments despite the Meta Compute cloud announcement. Evercore ISI reiterated an Outperform/$150 target on CoreWeave, noting the $21B Meta contract has take-or-pay protections through December 2032 — Meta cannot redirect spend without penalty. Nebius fell 12%+ on July 1 alongside CoreWeave on the Meta cloud competition fear, reflecting broader AI cloud competition concerns.

items.headline: Meta One subscription live; Glasses 69.2% smart glasses share; capex $125–145B confirmed
items.detail: Meta One paid AI subscription is live across the Family of Apps: Plus at $7.99/month and Premium at $19.99/month, competing directly with ChatGPT Plus and Claude Pro. Meta Glasses (launched June 23 at $299–$399) show strong early demand with 69.2% smart glasses market share. Meta's 2026 capex guidance of $125–145 billion remains unchanged from its April 29 Q1 earnings update. AMD CEO confirmed MI450 samples are shipping to Meta as part of the $60B/6GW multi-generation GPU partnership.

companies.name: Amazon / AWS
companies.summary: AWS Summit Washington DC wrapped July 1 having set the largest single-day federal AI infrastructure commitment in AWS history: a $1B IC Accelerated Modernization Framework, $1B Forward Deployed Engineering program, and AWS Secret Cloud for Industry. Amazon's FDE initiative predates Microsoft's $2.5B Frontier Co. by two days, reflecting an industry race to embed engineers with enterprise customers. GPT-5.6 Sol on Bedrock is expected in the July 10–17 window. Project Rainier runs nearly half a million Trainium2 chips with full Claude workload utilization resumed after Fable 5 restoration.

items.headline: AWS $1B FDE program mirrors Microsoft Frontier Co. — embedded-engineer race defines enterprise AI support
items.detail: Amazon's $1 billion Forward Deployed Engineering (FDE) organization — announced at AWS Summit DC on June 30 — places thousands of AWS engineers on-site with federal and enterprise customers to co-develop AI solutions and compress development timelines from months to days. The initiative predates Microsoft's $2.5B Frontier Co. (July 2) by two days. CIA Director John Ratcliffe confirmed CIA will use the IC Accelerated Modernization Framework (ICAMF, $1B) and that LLMs are already helping analysts surface hidden intelligence. AWS Secret Cloud for Industry (ASCI) gives defense contractors classified cloud access for the first time.

items.headline: Grok 4.3 live on Bedrock; GPT-5.6 Sol expected July 10–17 on Bedrock; AgentCore Web Search GA
items.detail: xAI's Grok 4.3 is on Amazon Bedrock with tool calling, structured output, and streaming. GPT-5.6 Sol is expected on Bedrock in the July 10–17 window alongside broader access rollout. Bedrock AgentCore Web Search — fully managed real-time web grounding for AI agents with cited knowledge — is generally available in US East (N. Virginia). This positions Bedrock as a leading multi-provider inference hub with Claude (Anthropic), GPT-5.5 (OpenAI), and Grok (xAI) all available.

items.headline: Anthropic-AWS $100B+/10-year deal active; Project Rainier at ~500K Trainium2 chips; Fable 5 restores full Claude on Bedrock
items.detail: The April 2026 expanded Anthropic-AWS agreement commits more than $100 billion over 10 years, securing up to 5GW of Trainium2/3/4 capacity. Project Rainier runs nearly half a million Trainium2 chips for Claude workloads. Fable 5 and Mythos 5 export controls were lifted July 1, restoring full Claude model access on Bedrock for the first time since June 12. Amazon invested $5 billion as a hyperscaler investor in the $65B Anthropic Series H. The Claude apps gateway (launched July 2) enables self-hosted enterprise control over Claude Code on Bedrock.

companies.name: xAI
companies.summary: Grok adult content concerns intensified after The Information reported it now accounts for the majority of platform activity, with web traffic down 22% from January to May. Grok 4.5 (1.5T parameters, V9 model) remains in private beta at Tesla and SpaceX. A 2T parameter model is in final training targeting late July. Both Colossus facilities remain fully committed to third parties. SPCX is trading around $170. The $60B Cursor acquisition remains on track for Q3 2026 close.

items.headline: Grok adult content now majority of platform activity — web traffic down 22%; CSAM unresolved
items.detail: The Information reported that adult content now accounts for the majority of Grok's platform activity. Grok web traffic dropped 22% between January and May per Similarweb — the largest decline of any major chatbot. xAI engineers acknowledged CSAM generation has no current technical fix, drawing regulatory scrutiny and a $530 million litigation reserve in SpaceX's IPO prospectus. Musk has positioned adult content as a differentiated feature, but the strategy limits enterprise adoption and creates headline risk ahead of broader Grok 4.5 deployment.

items.headline: Grok 4.5 (1.5T, V9) in private beta; 2T model late July; monthly training cadence planned through 2026
items.detail: Grok 4.5 — a 1.5 trillion parameter V9 foundation model trained in part on Cursor AI coding data — is running in private beta inside Tesla and SpaceX. xAI claims near-Opus 4.8 performance in internal evaluations; no independent benchmark has been published. A 2 trillion parameter successor is in final training with expected completion in late July 2026. xAI plans to release a new model trained from scratch every month for the remainder of 2026. The $60 billion Cursor acquisition (Anysphere) remains on track for Q3 2026 close.

items.headline: Both Colossus facilities fully committed — Reflection AI $150M/month started July 1; xAI own compute an open question
items.detail: SpaceX's $6.3 billion compute agreement with Reflection AI became active July 1 at $150 million per month for Colossus 2 in Memphis through 2029 (with a 90-day termination clause after the first 3 months). Combined with Colossus 1 serving Anthropic at approximately $1.25 billion per month, both xAI Colossus facilities are now fully committed to third-party tenants — raising open questions about xAI's own dedicated compute access for Grok model training as the 2T model moves toward completion.

companies.name: Broadcom
companies.category: AI networking, custom silicon, and infrastructure software
companies.summary: AVGO shares remain down approximately 16.5% over the past month from post-Q2 earnings disappointment. Q3 AI semiconductor revenue guidance stands at approximately $16B; the FY2027 $100B+ AI revenue target is unchanged. Jalapeño (OpenAI's first custom ASIC, co-developed with Broadcom in nine months) targets H2 2026 Stargate deployment. Six confirmed custom-silicon customers: Google, Meta, Microsoft, Anthropic, OpenAI, and one undisclosed. The Apollo/Blackstone $35B AI XPV Platform is operational, enabling 20GW+ of Broadcom XPU compute through 2028.

items.headline: Jalapeño LLM inference chip targets H2 2026 Stargate deployment — fastest ASIC development cycle on record
items.detail: OpenAI and Broadcom unveiled Jalapeño on June 24 — OpenAI's first custom AI accelerator, a reticle-sized ASIC designed for LLM inference on TSMC advanced process. Co-developed from design to tape-out in nine months — described as the fastest ASIC development cycle ever achieved in high-performance semiconductors. Jalapeño targets H2 2026 deployment in Stargate data centers. Broadcom CEO Hock Tan confirmed OpenAI as the sixth custom-silicon customer alongside Google, Meta, Microsoft, Anthropic, and one undisclosed. AI semiconductor revenue is now nearly 49% of Broadcom's total revenue.

items.headline: Q3 AI semiconductor revenue guidance ~$16B; FY2026 $56B; FY2027 target $100B+ unchanged
items.detail: Broadcom guided for approximately $16 billion in AI semiconductor revenue in Q3 FY2026 — a 48% sequential increase from Q2's $10.8 billion (+143% YoY). Full-year FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue guidance: $56 billion. FY2027 AI semiconductor revenue target: more than $100 billion. AVGO stock is down approximately 16.5% over the past month following maintained (not raised) full-year guidance. The fundamental AI demand picture remains strong — six custom-silicon customers and the Apollo/Blackstone XPV Platform as evidence.

items.headline: Apollo/Blackstone $35B AI XPV Platform operational — enables 20GW+ Broadcom XPU for Anthropic, OpenAI
items.detail: The Apollo/Blackstone $35B AI XPV Platform — operational since June 9 — enables more than 20 gigawatts of global AI compute capacity using Broadcom XPUs for hyperscalers including Anthropic and OpenAI through 2028. The platform facilitates Anthropic's 1+ gigawatt compute expansion at Fluidstack-based sites using Broadcom XPUs beginning mid-2026. Broadcom's VMware Cloud Foundation 9.1 supports production AI workloads with integrated security and mixed compute across AMD, Intel, and NVIDIA.

companies.name: Palantir
companies.summary: PLTR is trading at approximately $129.30 on July 3 — up from $125.73 at the July 1 close following the NVIDIA Sovereign AI Operating System announcement. President Trump's financial disclosure revealed he holds at least $1 million in Palantir shares and recently purchased more. The Army NGC2 Foundry deployment is advancing to broad deployment. Q1 2026 revenue was $1.632B (+84.7% YoY) — the highest growth rate in company history. Full-year guidance raised to approximately 71% growth.

items.headline: PLTR at ~$129.30 on July 3 — Trump $1M+ stake disclosed; Sovereign AI OS momentum continues
items.detail: Palantir Technologies is trading at approximately $129.30 on July 3, up from $125.73 on the July 1 close following the NVIDIA Sovereign AI Operating System announcement. President Trump's financial disclosure revealed he holds at least $1 million in Palantir shares and recently purchased more, adding a political-endorsement dimension to investor sentiment. PLTR had hit a 52-week low of $107.27 on June 25 before recovering. Analyst consensus: 21 Buy, 2 Sell with strong institutional support.

items.headline: Army NGC2 advancing to broad deployment; 2026 federal wins total $11.3B+; Q1 revenue $1.632B
items.detail: The U.S. Army picked Palantir Foundry as the cloud data layer for NGC2 (Next Generation Command and Control), moving from prototype to broad deployment. Major 2026 federal contract wins: $10B 10-year Army enterprise AI services; $1B 5-year DHS AI platform; $300M USDA deal (April); Treasury Department contract (June 23). Q1 2026 U.S. commercial revenue reached $595M (+133% YoY). Q1 revenue $1.632B (+84.7% YoY). Full-year guidance raised to approximately 71% growth.

items.headline: CEO Karp: AI industry 'effing insane' — Sovereign AI OS positions air-gapped Nemotron as alternative
items.detail: Palantir CEO Alex Karp called the AI industry 'effing insane' in a CNBC interview on July 1, accusing frontier AI firms of overcharging, exploiting customer data, and jeopardizing U.S. national security. The Palantir-NVIDIA Sovereign AI Operating System — running NVIDIA Nemotron open models on air-gapped Blackwell Ultra for U.S. government agencies — launched June 29 as Palantir's answer to frontier API consumption. Agencies own model weights and training data on their own hardware. The partnership drove PLTR to an all-time valuation high on July 1.

companies.name: Apple
companies.summary: iOS 27 developer beta continues with Gemini-powered Siri AI targeting September 2026 GA. Public beta is expected mid-July 2026. Gemini 3.5 Pro's continued delay as of July 3 maintains delivery risk for Apple's September iPhone launch window. OpenAI has retained outside counsel and is weighing a formal breach-of-contract notice over Siri placement. Apple is reportedly evaluating Mistral AI as an additional model option to hedge provider risk. The iOS 27 Extensions framework (user choice of ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini) is disabled pending EU DMA clarity.

items.headline: iOS 27 developer beta active; public beta mid-July; Gemini Siri AI GA September 2026
items.detail: iOS 27 developer beta — featuring a fully rebuilt Siri AI powered by a custom Google Gemini model (~1.2 trillion parameters) — has been running since WWDC June 8. Public beta is expected mid-July 2026. The rebuilt Siri includes a dedicated standalone app, Dynamic Island integration, full on-screen awareness, and multi-step task execution through a three-tier system: on-device, Private Cloud Compute, and Gemini for complex tasks. Apple pays approximately $1 billion per year for the Gemini license. iOS 27 Extensions framework (user choice of ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini) disabled pending EU DMA clarity. GA: September 2026.

items.headline: Gemini 3.5 Pro delay persists July 3 — prediction markets tracking; ongoing risk to September Siri AI launch
items.detail: Gemini 3.5 Pro remains unreleased as of July 3, maintaining delivery risk for Apple's September 2026 iPhone launch. Prediction markets are now tracking whether Gemini 3.5 Pro releases by July 31. Gemini 3.5 Flash is expected as a free/low-cost tier before Pro arrives. Apple's $1 billion/year Gemini deal makes the September timeline vulnerable to Google's internal quality delays, compounded by DeepMind's talent exodus. Apple is reportedly evaluating Mistral AI as an additional model option alongside Gemini as a multi-provider hedge.

items.headline: OpenAI breach-of-contract notice still pending; Apple evaluating Mistral as additional model
items.detail: OpenAI retained outside counsel and continues weighing a formal breach-of-contract notice against Apple over the lack of prime ChatGPT placement in Siri AI, alleging Apple made 'no honest effort' to meet integration commitments. No lawsuit has been filed as of July 3. Apple is evaluating Mistral AI as an additional model option alongside Gemini for Siri and iOS integration — reflecting a multi-provider hedging strategy to reduce reliance on any single AI partner across its 1.5B+ active device base.

companies.name: AMD
companies.category: AI accelerators, CPUs, and data center silicon
companies.summary: AMD Advancing AI 2026 is 19 days away (July 22–23 at Moscone Center, San Francisco). MI450 samples are confirmed shipping to lead customers OpenAI and Meta. Oracle announced a 50,000-unit MI450 deployment for the first publicly available MI450-based AI supercluster in Q3 2026. AMD's MI500 series roadmap (CDNA 6, 2nm, HBM4E) targets 1,000x performance vs. MI300X for 2027 production. Q2 2026 financial results are due August 4. MI400 series demand has already exceeded AMD's own internal 2027 projections.

items.headline: AMD Advancing AI 2026 — July 22–23 at Moscone Center; formal MI450 launch and MI500 preview expected
items.detail: AMD Advancing AI 2026 at Moscone Center in San Francisco on July 22–23 is the next major AMD milestone — 19 days away. CEO Lisa Su is expected to formally launch MI450, preview EPYC Venice (Zen 6) CPUs, confirm Helios rack-scale systems, and provide a first look at the MI500 series targeting 2027. Analysts project the MI400 series will generate approximately $7.2 billion in revenue in its first year. Q2 2026 financial results are due August 4.

items.headline: MI450 samples shipping to OpenAI and Meta; Oracle 50K unit MI450 supercluster in Q3 2026
items.detail: AMD CEO Lisa Su confirmed MI450 samples are shipping to lead customers OpenAI (6GW partnership, starting with 1GW MI450 in H2 2026) and Meta ($60B/6GW multi-generation agreement). Oracle announced a 50,000-unit AMD Instinct MI450 GPU deployment for the first publicly available MI450-based AI supercluster, with initial deployment in Q3 2026. MI450 features 432GB HBM4 memory, 20TB/s bandwidth, and 40 PFLOPs FP4 per GPU. Demand has already exceeded AMD's own internal 2027 projections.

items.headline: MI500 series revealed: CDNA 6, 2nm, HBM4E — targeting 1,000x performance vs MI300X for 2027
items.detail: AMD revealed the MI500 series roadmap targeting up to a 1,000x increase in AI performance compared to the MI300X introduced in 2023. The MI500 series uses next-generation CDNA 6 architecture, 2nm process technology, and HBM4E memory. CEO Lisa Su confirmed active customer engagement on MI500. The MI500 series targets 2027 production, positioning AMD to compete against NVIDIA's successor to Vera Rubin.

companies.name: Perplexity AI
companies.summary: Perplexity launched 'Perplexity for Gov' alongside the U.S. General Services Administration and is pursuing FedRAMP compliance, expanding into the federal market. Windows Personal Computer with hybrid local-cloud inference orchestrator is targeting July 2026 — the Mac version has been GA since May 7. A fake Perplexity Chrome extension discovered June 29 is keylogging users' searches. ARR: $500M (+335% YoY); 100M+ monthly active users; 92% Fortune 500 usage; $20B valuation. 2028 IPO target reaffirmed.

items.headline: Perplexity for Gov launched alongside GSA — FedRAMP compliance initiatives underway
items.detail: Perplexity launched 'Perplexity for Gov' alongside the U.S. General Services Administration, expanding into the federal AI search and research market. The company is pursuing FedRAMP compliance certification. This positions Perplexity alongside Palantir and AWS in competing for federal AI budgets as the government AI market expands following AWS Summit DC's ICAMF announcement. Perplexity now counts 92% of Fortune 500 companies as users, and has forged partnerships with Samsung, Deutsche Telekom, Vivo, and Airtel.

items.headline: Windows Personal Computer + hybrid inference orchestrator targeting July 2026
items.detail: Perplexity Personal Computer is generally available on Mac since May 7 for all Pro users. The Windows version with a hybrid local-cloud inference orchestrator is targeting July 2026. The orchestrator automatically routes tasks: sensitive data stays on-device, compute-intensive tasks go to frontier cloud models. Compatible with Intel Core Ultra Series 3 and NVIDIA RTX Spark hardware. Comet Enterprise browser is available with MDM deployment. CoreWeave's multi-year inference partnership uses dedicated NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 clusters, with Rubin systems rolling in H2 2026.

items.headline: Fake Perplexity Chrome extension keylogging users — security threat active since June 29
items.detail: Microsoft's Defender Security Research Team discovered a malicious Chrome extension called 'Search for perplexity ai' impersonating Perplexity AI while secretly recording users' search activity. The extension has been present since June 29. Users who installed it need to remove it manually. The threat does not affect Perplexity's core services but represents a brand spoofing risk as the company's consumer recognition grows. Perplexity is at $500M ARR, 100M+ monthly active users, and $20B valuation.

companies.name: CoreWeave
companies.summary: CRWV fell further to approximately $81.75 on July 3 — continuing the 14% decline from July 1 rather than recovering. Digitimes noted Meta's 2026 supply chain demand commitments are unchanged despite the Meta Compute cloud announcement. Analyst average Buy rating with $143.41 price target remains intact across 37 analysts. Revenue backlog: $99.4B (+50% QoQ). FY2026 revenue guidance: $12–13B. ARIA (AI Research & Iteration Agent) entered public preview on June 30. Full Anthropic Claude traffic is running on CoreWeave Vera Rubin NVL72 clusters following Fable 5 restoration.

items.headline: CRWV at ~$81.75 on July 3 — recovery stalled; Digitimes notes 2026 demand outlook unchanged
items.detail: CoreWeave shares are trading at approximately $81.75 on July 3, continuing a decline from $85.68 on July 1 rather than recovering. Digitimes reported on July 3 that server supply chain contacts are not seeing changes to Meta's 2026 shipment and revenue commitments despite the Meta Compute cloud announcement. Evercore ISI reiterated Outperform/$150, noting the $21B Meta take-or-pay contract through 2032 protects near-term revenue. The 37-analyst Buy consensus with $143.41 average target represents a significant discount to CRWV's current price.

items.headline: ARIA AI Research Agent in public preview; full Anthropic Claude traffic resumed on Vera Rubin NVL72
items.detail: CoreWeave launched ARIA (AI Research & Iteration Agent) into public preview on June 30, built into Weights & Biases using W&B Weave. ARIA processes thousands of experiment runs in minutes, surfacing insights and recommending model improvements. W&B Weave agent development capabilities also entered general availability. Fable 5's global restoration on July 1 means full Anthropic Claude inference traffic is running on CoreWeave's Vera Rubin NVL72 clusters for the first time since June 12.

items.headline: Revenue backlog $99.4B (+50% QoQ); FY2026 guidance $12–13B; NVIDIA holds ~11% equity stake
items.detail: CoreWeave reaffirmed FY2026 revenue guidance of $12–13 billion with an $18–19 billion exit run rate. Revenue backlog surged 50% sequentially to $99.4 billion, anchored by the $21B Meta agreement (December 2032) and major contracts with Anthropic, Perplexity, and nine of the top ten AI model providers. 2026 capex floor: $31 billion. Jane Street committed approximately $6 billion to CoreWeave's platform. NVIDIA holds approximately an 11% equity stake.

companies.name: Mistral AI
companies.summary: Mistral AI's 40MW facility at Bruyères-le-Châtel near Paris has been training models since early 2026; the full 44MW NVIDIA GB300-powered expansion has not officially launched as of July 3 after missing its end-of-June target. A secondary Les Ulis (Essonne) 10MW inference facility targets Q3 2026. Equity funding discussions are ongoing at approximately €20B ($23.2B) valuation targeting a €3B raise. Apple is reportedly evaluating Mistral as an additional model option alongside Gemini for Siri integration.

items.headline: Bruyères-le-Châtel 40MW training active; full 44MW GB300 facility still pending official launch
items.detail: Mistral AI's existing 40MW data center facility at Bruyères-le-Châtel near Paris has been training models since early 2026. The full 44MW NVIDIA GB300-powered expansion — funded by $830M in debt raised in March 2026 and operated by Eclairion, providing 13,800 GB300 GPUs and approximately 276 exaflops of FP4 compute — has not officially launched as of July 3 after missing its end-of-June target. A secondary Les Ulis (Essonne) 10MW inference facility is scheduled for Q3 2026. Mistral Compute sovereign GPU cloud launched at VivaTech 2026 on June 20.

items.headline: €3B equity raise at ~€20B ($23.2B) valuation in discussions; €1B 2026 revenue target
items.detail: Mistral AI is in discussions to raise approximately €3 billion ($3.5 billion) at a potential valuation near €20 billion ($23.2 billion), per Bloomberg. CEO Arthur Mensch targets €1 billion in 2026 revenue, up from €200 million in 2025. Industrial launch customers for the Vibe agent platform include Airbus, BMW Group, and ASML. Mistral is a founding member of the NVIDIA Nemotron Coalition. Combined European infrastructure targets: 200MW by end of 2027, including a €1.2B Sweden investment.

items.headline: Apple evaluating Mistral as additional Siri model option — potential major distribution catalyst
items.detail: Reports indicate Apple is evaluating Mistral AI as an additional model option alongside Gemini for Siri and iOS integration, reflecting Apple's multi-provider hedging strategy amid Gemini 3.5 Pro delivery delays. A Mistral-Apple distribution arrangement would be significant given Apple's installed base of more than 1.5 billion active devices. No official announcement has been made. Mistral's European sovereign AI positioning may also align with Apple's need for regulatory diversity across EU markets.

companies.name: Tesla
companies.category: Autonomous vehicles, robotics, and applied AI
companies.summary: Tesla reported Q2 2026 deliveries of 480,126 vehicles on July 2 — massively beating the 406,024 consensus (18% upside) and setting the best Q2 in company history. TSLA fell 7.49% despite the beat on 'buy the rumor, sell the news' dynamics — its worst day in almost a year. Q2 financial results are due July 22 after market close. Optimus Gen 3 production begins late July/August targeting 50,000 units. Cybercab fleet spans ~60–100 units across four cities with an under-$30K customer price targeted within six months.

items.headline: Tesla Q2 deliveries: 480,126 vs. 406,024 consensus — best Q2 ever; TSLA fell 7.49% on sell-the-news
items.detail: Tesla reported 480,126 vehicle deliveries in Q2 2026 — 18% above the company-compiled consensus of 406,024 and 25% above year-ago levels. The quarter is Tesla's best Q2 ever, surpassing the previous Q2 record of 466,140 set in 2023. Model 3/Y: 467,762 deliveries; other models (including Cybercab): 12,364. Energy storage: 13.5 GWh deployed. TSLA stock fell 7.49% on July 2 — its worst day in almost a year — on 'buy the rumor, sell the news' dynamics. Q2 financial results due July 22.

items.headline: Optimus Gen 3 production begins late July/August — 50,000 unit goal; external B2B customers late 2026
items.detail: Tesla's Optimus Gen 3 humanoid robot production is targeting late July/August 2026 with a full-year goal of 50,000 units. Several thousand Optimus units are already operating in Tesla's own factories. First external commercial B2B customers are targeted for late 2026. Tesla's Fremont facility is converting Model S/X lines to robot assembly while Giga Texas North Campus targets 10 million robots per year long-term. Tesla's Terafab initiative targets in-house semiconductor fabrication for the AI5 chip.

items.headline: Cybercab fleet ~60–100 units across 4 cities; under $30K in 6 months; BYD Q2 at 557K outpaces Tesla
items.detail: The Cybercab active fleet spans approximately 60–100+ units across Austin, Dallas, Houston, and the SF Bay Area with FSD v14.3 deployed (20% reduced inference latency). A Tesla executive confirmed the Cybercab will be priced under $30,000 for customers within six months. EPA filing (June 19) confirmed: 47.6 kWh battery, 163 kW motor, 418-mile unadjusted range. Grok 4.5 (1.5T parameter V9 model) is running in private beta inside Tesla. BYD delivered approximately 557,000 pure-EVs in Q2 2026, outpacing Tesla again for the quarter.

ipoTracker.status: October 2026 Nasdaq IPO target; Series H $65B at $965B post-money; ARR $47B; Claude apps gateway and Sonnet 5 strengthen pre-IPO product narrative; Fable 5 export risk removed July 1
ipoTracker.process: Anthropic confirmed its confidential draft Form S-1 submission to the SEC on June 1, 2026, targeting an October 2026 Nasdaq IPO led by Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, and Morgan Stanley with an expected raise of $60B+. The $65B Series H closed at a $965B post-money valuation, led by Altimeter Capital, Dragoneer, Greenoaks, and Sequoia Capital, with $15B from hyperscalers including $5B from Amazon, plus Micron, Samsung, and SK hynix. ARR: $47B as of May 2026. Fable 5 export controls lifted July 1 removed the largest pre-IPO operational risk that emerged in June. Claude Sonnet 5 launched July 1 as new Free/Pro default. Claude apps gateway launched July 2. Claude Science grants open through July 15.
ipoTracker.note: Claude apps gateway launch and Claude Enterprise analytics updates add enterprise infrastructure depth to the pre-IPO product narrative. October 2026 Nasdaq target remains intact.

latestArticle.title: Anthropic Adds Enterprise Gateway to Simplify Claude Code Access on AWS and Google Cloud

ipoTracker.status: GPT-5.6 Sol/Terra/Luna restricted to ~20 orgs (day 7); broader access expected July 10–17; IPO leaning 2027; 42-state AG probe ongoing; ChatGPT market share 46.4% — first below 50%
ipoTracker.process: OpenAI filed a confidential S-1 with the SEC in May/June 2026, with Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and JPMorgan leading. The company is leaning toward a 2027 listing — CFO Sarah Friar cited operational readiness, Stargate cash burn, and SpaceX IPO volatility as reasons not to rush. GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna remain restricted to approximately 20 government-vetted organizations as of July 3; broader access expected July 10–17. A coalition of 42 state AGs opened a formal probe June 12. ChatGPT's global AI assistant market share fell to 46.4% (Gemini at 27.7%, Claude at 10.3%). Revenue: approximately $2B per month; projected $14B operating loss 2026.
ipoTracker.note: July 10–17 broader GPT-5.6 access and Cerebras Sol deployment are the next positive catalysts. Frontier Co. adds a Microsoft enterprise channel. 2027 IPO posture remains the working assumption.

latestArticle.title: Previewing GPT-5.6 Sol: a next-generation model

ipoTracker.status: SPCX ~$170; Reflection AI $150M/month Colossus 2 payments active since July 1; Grok 4.5 private beta; $60B Cursor Q3 close on track; adult content and CSAM concerns unresolved
ipoTracker.process: SpaceX priced at $135/share on June 11, raising $75 billion at a $1.75 trillion valuation in the largest IPO ever recorded. Shares peaked at $225.64 on June 16 and pulled back to around $170 by July 3. Reflection AI's $150M/month Colossus 2 deal started July 1 — both Colossus facilities are now committed to third parties. Grok 4.5 (1.5 trillion parameters, V9 model) in private beta at Tesla and SpaceX. A 2T model is in final training expected to complete late July. The $60B Cursor acquisition is on track for Q3 2026 close. Grok adult content concerns and CSAM regulatory risk are now embedded in the SpaceX IPO prospectus ($530M litigation reserve).
ipoTracker.confidence: Confirmed; public company as of June 12, 2026
ipoTracker.note: Adult content and CSAM issues are the key new reputational risks since IPO. Both Colossus facilities commercialized. Cursor Q3 close remains the next major positive catalyst.

partnershipTracker.type: Sovereign AI Operating System — government and defense deployment
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; announced June 29; PLTR at ~$129.30 on July 3; Trump $1M+ stake disclosed; Nemotron open models on air-gapped Blackwell Ultra for U.S. agencies
partnershipTracker.terms: Palantir and NVIDIA announced the Sovereign AI Operating System on June 29 — a combined hardware and software platform enabling U.S. government agencies to deploy NVIDIA Nemotron open models in air-gapped Blackwell Ultra environments. Agencies own the model weights and training data on their own hardware. The partnership drove PLTR to an all-time valuation high on July 1 at $125.73; PLTR is at approximately $129.30 on July 3. President Trump's financial disclosure reveals he holds at least $1 million in Palantir shares.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by both companies
partnershipTracker.note: Trump stake disclosure adds a political-endorsement dimension to the Sovereign AI OS narrative for federal procurement. Sovereign AI OS uses Nemotron open models — distinct from the Anthropic-Palantir-AWS relationship which uses Claude.

latestArticle.title: Palantir Launches Engine for Deploying NVIDIA Nemotron Open Models in Sovereign Environments

partnershipTracker.status: Strained — Gemini deal sidelined OpenAI placement; breach-of-contract notice still under consideration; iOS 27 Extensions disabled; no lawsuit filed as of July 3
partnershipTracker.terms: Apple and OpenAI maintain an existing integration bringing ChatGPT into Siri and Apple Intelligence. Apple's Google Gemini deal (WWDC June 8) displaced OpenAI as the primary Siri AI engine. The iOS 27 Extensions framework (user choice of ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini in Siri) is disabled pending EU DMA clarity. OpenAI retained outside counsel and is weighing a formal breach-of-contract notice alleging Apple made 'no honest effort' to meet integration commitments. No lawsuit has been filed as of July 3.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed integration; breach-of-contract claim reported but not company-confirmed
partnershipTracker.note: OpenAI formal breach-of-contract notice is the key pending legal development. Apple evaluating Mistral adds further competitive pressure on OpenAI placement in the iOS ecosystem.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI considering legal action against Apple over Siri integration

partnershipTracker.type: AI model integration — Gemini powering Siri AI
partnershipTracker.status: Phase 1 active; full deployment September 2026; Gemini 3.5 Pro still delayed July 3 — prediction market now tracking; highest-priority delivery risk
partnershipTracker.terms: Apple struck a reported $1 billion per year deal for Google Gemini to power a rebuilt Siri AI, announced at WWDC 2026 (June 8). Phase 1 is in iOS 26.5; full Gemini Siri launches with iOS 27 in September 2026. Gemini 3.5 Pro remains unreleased as of July 3 with no confirmed date — the highest-priority delivery risk for the Apple-Google relationship heading into Q3. Prediction markets are now tracking the July 31 deadline.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Reported by credible outlets; not officially confirmed by Apple or Google
partnershipTracker.note: Gemini 3.5 Pro delay is the highest-priority watch item for this relationship. Prediction market tracking July 31 deadline. No confirmed date as of today.

latestArticle.title: Google delays Gemini 3.5 Pro to July as talent exodus deepens

partnershipTracker.type: AI compute and cloud GPU partnership
partnershipTracker.status: Active; MI300X on Azure; Frontier Co. launch drives additional Azure AI compute demand; AMD Advancing AI July 22–23
partnershipTracker.terms: AMD MI300X GPU instances are deployed on Azure for AI inference workloads. Microsoft Frontier Co. ($2.5B, 6,000 embedded engineers) launched July 2, driving additional Azure AI workload demand from enterprise customers. Copilot Cowork billing activated July 1. M365 commercial pricing increases took effect July 1. AMD Advancing AI 2026 (July 22–23) may include additional Azure-related deployment disclosures.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Ongoing relationship; no new announced terms
partnershipTracker.note: Frontier Co. launch adds enterprise professional services demand for Azure AI compute including AMD GPU workloads. AMD Advancing AI July 22–23 is the next potential milestone.

latestArticle.title: Microsoft commits $2.5 billion and 6,000 employees to new AI implementation unit

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed $60B, ~6GW multi-year; MI450 samples shipping to Meta; Meta Compute cloud may accelerate AMD GPU deployments for external workloads; capex updated to $125–145B
partnershipTracker.terms: AMD and Meta's 6-gigawatt, roughly $60 billion strategic partnership covers multiple generations of AMD Instinct GPUs co-developed via the Helios architecture through the Open Compute Project. AMD CEO confirmed MI450 samples are shipping to Meta. The agreement includes a 160-million-share AMD warrant giving Meta a potential 10% stake. Meta's 2026 capex guidance was raised to $125–145 billion in April. Meta Compute cloud business may accelerate AMD-Meta GPU deployments as Meta scales external cloud offerings.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by both companies; samples shipping per AMD CEO
partnershipTracker.note: Meta capex updated to $125–145B from prior $115–135B guidance. Meta Compute announcement may accelerate AMD GPU deployments at scale. AMD Advancing AI July 22–23 may include Meta deployment specifics.

latestArticle.title: AMD and Meta Announce Expanded Strategic Partnership to Deploy 6 Gigawatts of AMD GPUs

partnershipTracker.type: Infrastructure equity investment and multi-generational GPU supply
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed deep strategic partnership; CRWV at ~$81.75 on July 3 — recovery stalled; Digitimes notes 2026 demand unchanged; Fable 5 restored — full Anthropic Claude traffic to CoreWeave resumed
partnershipTracker.terms: NVIDIA invested roughly $2 billion in CoreWeave Class A common stock (~11% stake) and committed to accelerating more than 5GW of AI factories by 2030. CoreWeave completed the industry-first Vera Rubin NVL72 bring-up on June 1. Backlog $99.4 billion (+50% QoQ). CRWV at approximately $81.75 on July 3, continuing the July 1 decline. Digitimes noted on July 3 that Meta's 2026 supply chain demand commitments are unchanged. Fable 5 restoration means full Anthropic Claude traffic resumes on CoreWeave Vera Rubin NVL72 clusters.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by both companies
partnershipTracker.note: Meta cloud announcement is the key negative development for CRWV stock but does not change the GPU supply relationship with NVIDIA or contracted backlog. Digitimes July 3 supply chain comment is the key near-term positive signal.

latestArticle.title: Meta compute plan revives AI bubble worries, but server suppliers stay upbeat

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; Mistral Compute sovereign GPU cloud on 18K Grace Blackwell systems live; Bruyères-le-Châtel 40MW training active; full GB300 expansion pending; Apple evaluating Mistral
partnershipTracker.terms: NVIDIA and Mistral AI launched Mistral Compute — a sovereign AI GPU cloud platform built on 18,000 NVIDIA Grace Blackwell systems for European customers and governments — at VivaTech 2026 (June 20). Mistral is a founding member of the NVIDIA Nemotron Coalition. Mistral's 40MW facility at Bruyères-le-Châtel has been training models since early 2026; the full 44MW GB300 expansion has not officially launched as of July 3. Apple is reportedly evaluating Mistral for Siri/iOS integration.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by both companies at VivaTech
partnershipTracker.note: Full 44MW GB300 data center launch expected in early July. Apple's Mistral evaluation is a potential major new distribution relationship.

latestArticle.title: France Advances Europe's AI Future With NVIDIA Technologies

partnershipTracker.type: AI compute contract — Colossus 2 data center access
partnershipTracker.status: Active as of July 1 — $150M/month payments began; Colossus 2 dedicated to Reflection AI through 2029
partnershipTracker.terms: SpaceX's $6.3 billion compute agreement with Reflection AI (valued at $25 billion, working with DoE Genesis Mission and Pentagon AI) is now active as of July 1 at $150 million per month for NVIDIA GB300 GPU access via Colossus 2 in Memphis through 2029, with a 90-day termination clause after the first 3 months. Both Colossus 1 (Anthropic) and Colossus 2 (Reflection AI) are now fully committed to third parties.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by CNBC reporting; payments began July 1
partnershipTracker.note: July 1 payments began. With both Colossus facilities committed to third parties, xAI's own future dedicated compute access for Grok model training remains an open question as the 2T model nears completion.

latestArticle.title: SpaceX AI Colossus data center signs deal with Reflection AI

partnershipTracker.type: Structured finance platform for Broadcom AI XPU deployment
partnershipTracker.status: Operational — $35B capital solution; enables 20GW+ Broadcom XPU compute; six custom silicon customers confirmed including OpenAI (Jalapeño); AVGO down ~16.5% past month
partnershipTracker.terms: Apollo and Blackstone lead a $35 billion capital solution for the Broadcom AI XPV Platform, enabling more than 20 gigawatts of global AI compute capacity using Broadcom XPUs for hyperscalers including Anthropic and OpenAI through 2028. The platform is operational since June 9. Jalapeño (the OpenAI-Broadcom custom chip unveiled June 24) confirms OpenAI as Broadcom's sixth custom-silicon customer. AVGO is down approximately 16.5% over the past month — does not change the platform terms.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by Apollo press release and Broadcom announcement
partnershipTracker.note: AVGO stock decline is market-driven. Six custom-silicon customers confirmed. FY2027 $100B+ AI revenue target is the long-term anchor.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI and Broadcom reveal Jalapeño, first AI chip in partnership
